title: scurrula lepidota
intro: Scurrula lepidota, also known as the poison tree, is a type of plant native to Southeast Asia that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The plant's medicinal properties include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Scurrula lepidota include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. In traditional herbal preparations, Scurrula lepidota is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are typically applied externally to treat skin conditions and wounds.
